headline: Spread Compression & Positioning Risk Define G10 Regimes; ECB Decision Looms

sections.macro_context: Rate differential compression is the dominant macro driver across G10 today. US-DE 10Y spreads have tightened 14bp in one week to 117bp, while US-JP 10Y spreads compressed 7bp to 185bp. This compression mechanically favors foreign currency strength, yet positioning remains crowded short in both EUR and JPY, creating asymmetric squeeze risk. The ECB meeting tomorrow (17 April) is the pivotal catalyst—any hawkish surprise or hawkish guidance would challenge the current EUR strength narrative. Volatility in EUR/USD sits at the 87th percentile, suggesting positioning signals are less reliable and may already be pricing an ECB move. Across G10, regimes remain NEUTRAL, but with elevated tail risk in both directional anchors.

eurusd.narrative: EUR/USD closed at 1.1799, up 26bp on the day and +417bp year-to-date. The regime is supported by spread compression: the US-DE 10Y differential has narrowed 60bp over 12 months and sits at just 117bp today—a historically tight level that favors EUR strength on rate differentials alone. However, the critical risk is positioning: asset managers are at the 1st percentile crowded short (258,050 contracts, +33.3% of OI), and leveraged money is at the 11th percentile short as well. With EUR/USD volatility elevated at 87th percentile, the market is priced for event risk. The ECB decision tomorrow will determine whether this positioning compresses further or unwinds.
eurusd.key_driver: US-DE 10Y spread at +117bp with asset managers crowded short at 1st percentile creates squeeze asymmetry ahead of ECB.
eurusd.watch_for: ECB dovish surprise (rate hold with easing bias) would trigger short covering; hawkish guidance would cap the rally. Break above R2 at 1.2018 signals momentum shift toward EUR strength.

usdjpy.narrative: USD/JPY trades at 158.79, down 27bp on the day but +1087bp year-to-date. Spread compression favors lower USD/JPY: the US-JP 10Y differential has compressed 115bp over 12 months and sits at 185bp today. Carry trade positioning is partially intact—leveraged money is at the neutral 42nd percentile short, suggesting the 2024 unwind is complete and some carry longs have reaccumulated. Asset managers, however, remain crowded short at 1st percentile. The BoJ path is the key variable: any BoJ tightening or hawkish forward guidance would reinforce lower USD/JPY momentum. Volatility is normal at 23rd percentile, so positioning signals remain more reliable here than in EUR/USD.
usdjpy.key_driver: US-JP 10Y spread at +185bp compressed 115bp in 12 months; carry trade partially recovered but asset managers crowded short at 1st percentile.
usdjpy.watch_for: BoJ hawkish messaging or next policy tightening would accelerate the lower USD/JPY move. Support at 157.92 (S1) is key; break below signals continuation toward 152.45.

usdinr.narrative: USD/INR regimes remain NEUTRAL and directionally unreliable due to active RBI intervention. The RBI has deployed -4.7B USD in reserves over the past 7 days to defend the INR floor. The INR composite score is +6.4 (neutral), with RBI support contributing -6 to the score—this reflects the intervention drag on any natural weakening pressure. Gold correlation has broken (USD/INR showing -247 correlation instead of the expected positive import-demand signal), partly due to seasonal Akshaya Tritiya buying masking fundamental flows. Oil correlation is near zero at 61bp, indicating the traditional oil-import channel is also subdued. With USD/INR volatility at the 100th percentile, entry precision is not warranted.
usdinr.key_driver: RBI actively defending INR floor with -4.7B USD intervention in past 7 days; gold and oil correlation channels broken.
usdinr.watch_for: RBI reserve depletion rate and any shift in intervention intensity; if RBI support weakens, USD/INR could reprrice higher. Monitor Akshaya Tritiya seasonal flows (mid-April peak) for dissipation.
